A.evidence: Most prose is unreadable glyph/codepoint garbage like /a116; Table 1 has row/column shape but cell text is gibberish, while order roughly follows pages.

B.evidence: Readable prose where present, but skips intro body and Table 1 entirely, jumps from Fig. 1 to Fig. 2/section 2, and has OCR artifacts like turingGLYPH.

C.evidence: Very faithful prose and order with usable headings, captions, footnotes, degree/superscript notation; Table 1 is a clean Markdown table though merged rows are represented with blank v2020 cells.

D.evidence: Mostly accurate text/order, but Table 1 is flattened line-by-line instead of tabular, and symbols degrade in places such as 45' and 155'C.

E.evidence: Good order and Markdown table, but recurring ligature/OCR losses such as fth/gure/specications/dierent and minor table/header artifacts reduce fidelity.

F.evidence: After the title it hallucinates many repeated AI-driven robot lines and produces a massively mangled table separator, making content largely unusable.

G.evidence: Faithful prose, captions, image placeholders, hierarchy, and structurally correct HTML Table 1; only small OCR/name/math issues like Sabole Jut and $30A^3$.

H.evidence: Early text and HTML table are good, but raw layout JSON/bboxes are inserted mid-document and the Kicker section heading/intro before Fig. 6 are missing.
